name: Just and Long-suffering Job
troparia: (Tone 1) When the enemy of the just beheld the riches of Job's virtues,* he strove to seize them.* He laid waste the tower of his body* but could not touch the treasures of his spirit.* For the soul of the blameless one was armed,* yet I have been stripped and bound by the enemy.** But before I die, deliver me from evil and save me, O Saviour!
kontakia: (Tone 8) O faithful Job, celebrated servant of God,* you were a deeply just man and God’s sincere worshiper.* You gave the world a stirring lesson by your patience and long-suffering.* Therefore, we sing a hymn of praise to your memory and we honour you.
